name: BenevolentAI
tagline: AI-powered drug discovery using knowledge graphs and deep learning
description: BenevolentAI raised $282M and was valued at £2B for AI drug discovery. Despite correctly flagging baricitinib for COVID-19, it couldn't sustain public company costs and delisted in 2025.
story: Founded in London in 2013 by Ken Mulvany, BenevolentAI raised $282 million and was valued at up to £2 billion as one of the world's leading AI drug discovery companies. The company's knowledge graph technology scored a genuine scientific win by flagging baricitinib as a potential COVID-19 treatment — it received FDA Emergency Use Authorization. But one success couldn't sustain the enormous costs of being a publicly listed AI pharmaceutical company on Euronext Amsterdam. BenevolentAI laid off over 30% of staff, closed its US office, and reduced cash burn by 20% — still not enough. In March 2025, the company delisted and merged into private entity Osaka Holdings, effectively admitting the public experiment had failed. The science was real; the business model wasn't.
rejection_reason: Company still operating under private ownership (Osaka Holdings) as of April 2026 with projected runway into 2027. Delisted from stock exchange but not shut down.
review_flags: NEEDS RE-VERIFICATION: BenevolentAI is still operating as of April 2026. The company delisted from Euronext Amsterdam in March 2025 and merged into private entity Osaka Holdings, but it continues drug discovery operations with a cash runway projected into 2027. Delisting from a stock exchange is not the same as shutting down. The entry says shutdown 2025-03 but the company is alive under private ownership. Consider rejecting — the project criteria require the company to have actually shut down, not just gone private.
